title: Fraction Division Problem Solving
contentObjective: I can solve real-world problems by writing and solving fraction division equations.
languageObjective: I can explain my reasoning using the words model, equation, solution, and reasonableness.

vocabulary.definition: A visual or mathematical representation used to solve a problem
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una representación visual o matemática que se usa para resolver un problema
vocabulary.visual: A bar model showing 3/4 split into 1/8-size pieces, or the equation 3/4 ÷ 1/8 = 6
vocabulary.cloze: A drawing or diagram that shows a math situation is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math sentence that uses an equal sign to show two expressions have the same value
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una expresión matemática que usa un signo igual para mostrar que dos expresiones tienen el mismo valor
vocabulary.visual: 3/4 ÷ 1/8 = 6 — the left side (division) equals the right side (answer)
vocabulary.cloze: A math sentence that says two amounts are equal, with an = sign, is an ___.

vocabulary.definition: The answer to an equation or problem
vocabulary.definitionEs: La respuesta a una ecuación o problema
vocabulary.cloze: The value that makes an equation true is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: Checking whether an answer makes sense in the context of the problem
vocabulary.definitionEs: Revisar si una respuesta tiene sentido en el contexto del problema
vocabulary.visual: 3 ÷ 1/4 = 12. Is 12 reasonable? Yes, because 1/4 is small so many pieces fit into 3.
vocabulary.cloze: Checking whether an answer makes sense for the problem is checking its ___.

vocabulary.definition: Operations that undo each other, like multiplication and division
vocabulary.definitionEs: Operaciones que se deshacen entre sí, como la multiplicación y la división
vocabulary.cloze: Operations that undo each other, like multiplication and division, are ___.

launch.narrative: Agent Torres is closing the biggest case of the year. She has 2/3 of a gallon of fingerprint dust left and each test uses 1/6 of a gallon. Before the evidence expires, she needs to figure out exactly how many tests she can run. Can you write the equation and solve it?
launch.contextImage: Detective at a forensics station with vials of fingerprint dust and case files spread across the desk
noticePrompts: What is the total amount of fingerprint dust?
How much does each test use?
What operation will help solve this problem?
wonderPrompts: How do you decide which number is the dividend and which is the divisor?
How can you check if your answer is reasonable?

explore.instructions: Read each word problem. Set up the fraction division equation, solve it, and check for reasonableness.
explore.label: Set Up and Solve Fraction Division Equations

rows.problem: A detective has 3/4 pound of plaster. Each mold uses 1/8 pound. How many molds can she make?
rows.check: Yes — 6 > 3/4 because each piece is very small

rows.problem: A suspect traveled 5/6 of a mile. Each city block is 1/12 of a mile. How many blocks did the suspect travel?
rows.check: Yes — 10 > 5/6 because twelfths are smaller than sixths

rows.problem: An evidence bag holds 2/3 of a kilogram. Each sample weighs 1/9 of a kilogram. How many samples fit?
rows.check: Yes — 6 > 2/3 because ninths are much smaller than thirds

discourse.prompt: How do you decide which value is the dividend and which is the divisor in a word problem?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The total amount is the ___ because it is being split into groups. The size of each group is the ___ because we are dividing by that amount.

optional.stem: A 3/4-liter bottle fills cups that hold 1/8 liter. How many cups can it fill?
Tom has 4½ yards of rope and cuts ¾-yard pieces. How many pieces?
A trail is 7½ miles. A hiker rests every 1¼ miles. How many rest stops (not counting the start)?
You have 2/3 of a pizza and split it into 1/6-pizza slices. How many slices?

approaching.stem: A ribbon is 4/5 of a yard long. Each bow uses 1/10 of a yard. Which equation finds how many bows can be made?
approaching.explanation: The total (4/5 yard) is divided into groups of 1/10 yard each. The equation is 4/5 ÷ 1/10 = 4/5 × 10/1 = 40/5 = 8 bows.

approaching.stem: A detective has 1/2 gallon of solution. Each test uses 1/8 gallon. How many tests can be run?
A trail is 3/4 mile long. A jogger runs laps of 1/4 mile each. How many laps does the jogger complete?
approaching.explanation: 3/4 ÷ 1/4 = 3/4 × 4/1 = 12/4 = 3 laps. This is reasonable: three 1/4-mile pieces fit into 3/4 mile.

approaching.instructions: Sort each word problem: should you DIVIDE or MULTIPLY to solve it?

items.text: How many 1/3-cup scoops fit into 2 cups? (2 ÷ 1/3)
A ribbon is 3/4 yard. Each piece is 1/8 yard. How many pieces? (3/4 ÷ 1/8)
You run 1/2 mile, 3 times. How far total? (1/2 × 3)
A recipe uses 2/3 cup. You make half a recipe. How much? (2/3 × 1/2)

approaching.stem: A board is 5/6 foot long. Each shelf bracket needs 1/6 foot. How many brackets fit?

onLevel.label: Solve each word problem by writing and solving an equation.

rows.problem: A board is 7/8 foot long. Each shelf bracket needs 1/4 foot. How many brackets fit?
A jug holds 5/6 liter. Each cup holds 1/3 liter. How many cups can be filled?
A path is 3/4 mile long. A runner does laps of 3/8 mile. How many laps fit?

onLevel.instructions: Sort: is the equation and solution CORRECT, or is there an error?

items.text: A tank has 5/6 gallon. Each bucket holds 1/3 gallon. → 5/6 ÷ 1/3 = 2 1/2 buckets
A pipe is 2/3 foot long. Each piece is 1/6 foot. → 2/3 ÷ 1/6 = 4 pieces
A bag has 3/4 lb. Each scoop is 1/8 lb. → 3/4 ÷ 1/8 = 6 scoops
A rope is 1/2 yard. Each section is 1/4 yard. → 1/4 ÷ 1/2 = 1/2 section
A jug has 4/5 liter. Each cup is 2/5 liter. → 4/5 × 2/5 = 8/25 cups
A shelf is 7/8 foot. Each book is 1/4 foot. → 7/8 ÷ 1/4 = 7/32 books

onLevel.instructions: Use the bar model to solve: A detective has 3/4 pound of clay. Each mold needs 1/8 pound. How many molds?
onLevel.label: Model 3/4 ÷ 1/8
onLevel.questionText: How many 1/8-pound molds can be made from 3/4 pound?

onLevel.stem: Marcus solved 2/3 ÷ 1/4 and got 8/3 = 2 2/3. He says 'That can't be right because I started with less than 1.' Is Marcus's math correct? Is his reasoning correct?
choices: His math is correct (2 2/3), but his reasoning is wrong — dividing by a small fraction gives a larger result
His math and reasoning are both correct — the answer should be less than 1
His math is wrong — the answer should be 1/6
His math is wrong — the answer should be 8/12
onLevel.explanation: 2/3 ÷ 1/4 = 2/3 × 4/1 = 8/3 = 2 2/3. This IS correct. When you divide by a number less than 1, the quotient is greater than the dividend.

extending.title: Find the Problem-Solving Error

workedExample.label: Problem
workedExample.work: A tank has 3/4 gallon of water. Each bucket holds 1/6 gallon. How many buckets can be filled?

extending.correctWork: The equation should be 3/4 ÷ 1/6 (total ÷ group size). Then 3/4 × 6/1 = 18/4 = 4 1/2 buckets.
hints: Which value is the total being divided — 3/4 or 1/6?
The student wrote the dividend and divisor in the wrong order. The total (3/4) should come first.

extending.prompt: Write your own fraction division word problem where the answer is 8. Then show the equation, solve it step by step, and explain how you know your answer is reasonable.
extending.sentenceFrame: My word problem: ___. The equation is ___ ÷ ___ = ___. I solved it using KCF: ___ × ___ = ___. The answer 8 is reasonable because ___.

extending.label: Solve each multi-step problem. Show the equation and verify with multiplication.

rows.problem: A chef has 2 1/2 cups of flour. Each batch of cookies needs 1/4 cup. How many batches?
A detective has 1 1/3 gallons of solution. Each test uses 2/3 gallon. How many tests?
A board is 3 3/4 feet long. Each piece must be 5/8 foot. How many pieces?

connect.scenario: A forensic chemist has 5/6 of a liter of testing solution. Each DNA test requires 1/12 of a liter. She needs to figure out how many tests she can run before the solution runs out.
connect.promptQuestion: How does setting up a fraction division equation help solve this real-world problem?
connect.prompt: The chemist can run ___ tests because 5/6 ÷ 1/12 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A pipe is 3/4 meter long. Each connector piece is 3/8 meter. How many connectors fit on the pipe?
exitTicket.explanation: 3/4 ÷ 3/8 = 3/4 × 8/3 = 24/12 = 2. Two connector pieces fit on the pipe.
